title: Multiple sub-Nyquist Sampling Encoding (MUSE)
short description: Multiplite sub-Nyquist Sampling Encoding (MUSE) also known as Hi-Vision was a early analogue high-defition television standard developed by NHK Science and Technical Research Laboratories. Replaced by digital ISDB broadcast since 2007.
description:
**This Article is currently in stub process. Feel free to add on to it by adding accurate images, sound files, and IF-IQ, by hitting the "Edit" button above this page.**

**Multiplite sub-Nyquist Sampling Encoding (MUSE)** also known as Hi-Vision was a early analogue high-defition television standard developed by NHK Science and Technical Research Laboratories. Replaced by digital ISDB broadcast since 2007.
